Town or city where the business is located
URL to the business's cookie policy
ID of the `Country` record for this business
ID of the `Currency` used for billing at this business
Default language identifier for communications and UI
Primary email address displayed on the business profile
Total floor space of the business premises
Number of floors in the business premises
Display name of the business location
Full description shown on the Nexudus Passport listing
Display name shown on the Nexudus Passport listing
Whether this business is published and visible on Nexudus Passport
Tagline shown on the Nexudus Passport listing
Primary phone number displayed on the business profile
URL to the business's privacy policy
Highlight quote displayed on the business profile
Brief introduction shown on the business profile
State or province where the business is located
Street name component of the address
Street number component of the address
Comma-separated tags for categorising the business
Terms and conditions text for this business
ID of the timezone used for scheduling and display at this business
URL slug used for the business's public web address
Output JSON envelope with summary for AI agents
Override the Nexudus API base URL
Output JSON envelope with summary for AI agents
Override the Nexudus API base URL
Pricing plan IDs to add to the restriction list on update
Output JSON envelope with summary for AI agents
Allow sales to continue even when stock reaches zero
Whether to pro-rate the price when the product is added or removed part-way through a billing period
Whether the product is archived. Archived products cannot be sold but existing charges remain active
Controls whether the product can be sold as a one-off purchase, a recurring charge, or both (RecurrentOrOneOff, OnlyRecurrent, OnlyOneOff)
Override the Nexudus API base URL
ID of the location where this product is available
Automatically create a delivery record for the customer when this product is purchased
Tax rate ID applied when a customer is tax-exempt
Financial account ID used for accounting integration and revenue mapping
Whether to invoice the customer directly rather than their company or team
Custom text shown on the invoice line instead of the product name
Restrict purchase to contacts (customers without an active contract)
Restrict purchase to customers with an active contract (members)
Tax rate ID applied when a customer qualifies for a reduced rate
Pricing plan IDs to remove from the restriction list on update
Mark the product as featured or highlighted
Stock level at which a low-stock alert is triggered
Category of the product: DayPass, CreditBundle, Stationery, BookingFeature, BookingProducts, or Other
List of pricing plan (tariff) IDs. When set, only customers on one of these plans can purchase this product
Enable stock tracking for this product. When enabled, each sale reduces the stock count
Whether the product is visible to customers on the members portal and mobile app
Pricing plan IDs to add to the restriction list on update
Output JSON envelope with summary for AI agents
Allow sales to continue even when stock reaches zero
Whether to pro-rate the price when the product is added or removed part-way through a billing period
Whether the product is archived. Archived products cannot be sold but existing charges remain active
Controls whether the product can be sold as a one-off purchase, a recurring charge, or both (RecurrentOrOneOff, OnlyRecurrent, OnlyOneOff)
Override the Nexudus API base URL
Automatically create a delivery record for the customer when this product is purchased
Tax rate ID applied when a customer is tax-exempt
Financial account ID used for accounting integration and revenue mapping
Whether to invoice the customer directly rather than their company or team
Custom text shown on the invoice line instead of the product name
Restrict purchase to contacts (customers without an active contract)
Restrict purchase to customers with an active contract (members)
Tax rate ID applied when a customer qualifies for a reduced rate
Pricing plan IDs to remove from the restriction list on update
Mark the product as featured or highlighted
Stock level at which a low-stock alert is triggered
Category of the product: DayPass, CreditBundle, Stationery, BookingFeature, BookingProducts, or Other
List of pricing plan (tariff) IDs. When set, only customers on one of these plans can purchase this product
Enable stock tracking for this product. When enabled, each sale reduces the stock count
Whether the product is visible to customers on the members portal and mobile app

Command.Name: run-command
Command.Description: Execute a command on one or more products

Argument.Name: command-key
Argument.Description: The command key to execute

Argument.Name: ids
Argument.Description: Comma-separated entity IDs to run the command on

Option.Description: Output JSON envelope with summary for AI agents
Override the Nexudus API base URL
Command parameters in Name=Value format (can be specified multiple times)
Output JSON envelope with summary for AI agents
Override the Nexudus API base URL
Output JSON envelope with summary for AI agents
Override the Nexudus API base URL
Output JSON envelope with summary for AI agents
Override the Nexudus API base URL
Output JSON envelope with summary for AI agents
Override the Nexudus API base URL
Output JSON envelope with summary for AI agents
Override the Nexudus API base URL

Command.Name: run-command
Command.Description: Execute a command on one or more timepasses

Argument.Name: command-key
Argument.Description: The command key to execute

Argument.Name: ids
Argument.Description: Comma-separated entity IDs to run the command on

Option.Description: Output JSON envelope with summary for AI agents
Override the Nexudus API base URL
Command parameters in Name=Value format (can be specified multiple times)
Output JSON envelope with summary for AI agents
Override the Nexudus API base URL
Output JSON envelope with summary for AI agents
Override the Nexudus API base URL
Output JSON envelope with summary for AI agents
Override the Nexudus API base URL
Output JSON envelope with summary for AI agents
Override the Nexudus API base URL
Output JSON envelope with summary for AI agents
Override the Nexudus API base URL
Output JSON envelope with summary for AI agents
Override the Nexudus API base URL
Output JSON envelope with summary for AI agents
Override the Nexudus API base URL
Booking availability exception IDs to add (use on update to append without replacing).
Linked resource IDs to add (use on update to append without replacing).
Tariff IDs to add to the allowed list (use on update to append without replacing).
Team IDs to add to the allowed list (use on update to append without replacing).
Output JSON envelope with summary for AI agents
Amenity flag: resource has air conditioning.
Maximum number of attendees or concurrent bookings allowed. Used with AllowMultipleBookings to control capacity.
When true, overlapping bookings are permitted up to the Allocation capacity.
When true, the resource is archived and hidden from all views. It cannot be booked.
Override the Nexudus API base URL
Maximum number of days in advance a booking can be made for this resource. Null means no limit.
IDs of BookingAvailabilityException records that override normal availability (e.g., closures, special hours).
ID of the location (business) this resource belongs to. Set at creation and cannot be changed.
Fixed cancellation fee amount. Used when CancellationFeeType is Absolute.
Cancellation fee as a percentage of the booking cost. Used when CancellationFeeType is Percentage.
ID of the product used to invoice cancellation fees for this resource.
How the cancellation fee is calculated: Absolute (fixed amount) or Percentage (of booking cost).
Amenity flag: catering is available for this resource.
Amenity flag: resource has CCTV coverage.
When true, a fee is charged for late cancellations (past the LateCancellationLimit).
Set to true to remove the resource's existing picture.
Amenity flag: resource has a conference phone.
Free-text description shown to users when viewing the resource details.
Amenity flag: resource has a desktop monitor.
Sort position when listing resources. Lower values appear first.
Amenity flag: resource has a display screen.
Amenity flag: resource has a dual display screen.
Custom HTML or text included in booking confirmation emails for this resource.
Amenity flag: resource has a flip chart.
Optional grouping label used to cluster related resources together in the UI (e.g., 'Floor 2').
When true, this resource does not appear on the booking calendar view.
Amenity flag: resource has internet access.
Minimum interval (in minutes) between consecutive bookings on this resource, used as a buffer for setup or cleaning.
Amenity flag: resource has a large display.
Timestamp of the last cleaning event for this resource.
Minimum lead time (in minutes) required before a booking can start. Prevents last-minute bookings.
Cut-off in minutes before the booking start time. Cancellations after this point are considered late and may incur a fee.
GPS latitude coordinate of the resource's physical location.
When true, the total number of visitors added to a booking cannot exceed the Allocation capacity.
Comma-separated string of linked resource IDs (read-only alternative view of LinkedResources).
IDs of other resources that are automatically booked together with this one (e.g., a room and its attached breakout area).
GPS longitude coordinate of the resource's physical location.
Maximum allowed duration for a single booking on this resource, in minutes.
Minimum allowed duration for a single booking on this resource, in minutes.
Display name of the resource (e.g., 'Board Room A', 'Phone Booth 3').
Amenity flag: resource has natural light.
URL of a new image to set as the resource's picture. The image is downloaded and stored by the system.
Cooldown in minutes: prevents the same user from booking this specific resource again within this window after their last booking ends.
Cooldown in minutes: prevents the same user from booking any resource after booking this one, for the specified window.
Cooldown in minutes: prevents any user from booking this resource within the specified window after the previous booking ends.
When true, only contacts (non-member customers) can book this resource.
When true, only coworkers invoiced by this specific location can book this resource.
When true, only active members (coworkers with a plan) can book this resource.
Amenity flag: resource has a PA system.
Filename of the resource's current picture (read-only).
Amenity flag: resource has a privacy screen.
Amenity flag: resource has a projector.
Amenity flag: resource is located in a quiet zone.
Booking availability exception IDs to remove (use on update to remove without replacing).
Linked resource IDs to remove (use on update to remove without replacing).
Tariff IDs to remove from the allowed list (use on update to remove without replacing).
Team IDs to remove from the allowed list (use on update to remove without replacing).
Maximum time span (in months) over which a recurring booking series can extend.
Maximum number of occurrences allowed when creating a recurring booking for this resource.
When true, bookings for this resource are held as pending until an admin approves them.
ID of the ResourceType this resource belongs to. Determines which ExtraService pricing rules apply.
Amenity flag: resource has secure storage.
Amenity flag: resource has a security lock.
JSON-encoded shifts configuration defining the resource's availability schedule.
Amenity flag: resource has a standard phone.
Amenity flag: resource has a standing desk.
Built-in resource category used for system behaviour (e.g., MeetingRoom, HotDesk, PhoneBooth). Distinct from the custom ResourceType.
IDs of pricing plans (tariffs) whose members are allowed to book this resource. Empty means no plan restriction.
Amenity flag: tea and coffee are available.
IDs of teams whose members are allowed to book this resource. Empty means no team restriction.
When true, bookings for this resource use the location's shared Zoom account to create virtual meetings.
Amenity flag: resource has video conferencing equipment.
Whether the resource is visible and bookable by end users. Hidden resources can still be booked by admins.
Amenity flag: resource has a voice recorder.
Amenity flag: resource has a whiteboard.
Amenity flag: resource has a wireless charger.
Amenity flag: resource has wireless presentation capabilities.
Zoom user ID used to host virtual meetings when UseSharedZoomAccount is false.
Booking availability exception IDs to add (use on update to append without replacing).
Linked resource IDs to add (use on update to append without replacing).
Tariff IDs to add to the allowed list (use on update to append without replacing).
Team IDs to add to the allowed list (use on update to append without replacing).
Output JSON envelope with summary for AI agents
Amenity flag: resource has air conditioning.
Maximum number of attendees or concurrent bookings allowed. Used with AllowMultipleBookings to control capacity.
When true, overlapping bookings are permitted up to the Allocation capacity.
When true, the resource is archived and hidden from all views. It cannot be booked.
Override the Nexudus API base URL
Maximum number of days in advance a booking can be made for this resource. Null means no limit.
IDs of BookingAvailabilityException records that override normal availability (e.g., closures, special hours).
Fixed cancellation fee amount. Used when CancellationFeeType is Absolute.
Cancellation fee as a percentage of the booking cost. Used when CancellationFeeType is Percentage.
ID of the product used to invoice cancellation fees for this resource.
How the cancellation fee is calculated: Absolute (fixed amount) or Percentage (of booking cost).
Amenity flag: catering is available for this resource.
Amenity flag: resource has CCTV coverage.
When true, a fee is charged for late cancellations (past the LateCancellationLimit).
Set to true to remove the resource's existing picture.
Amenity flag: resource has a conference phone.
Free-text description shown to users when viewing the resource details.
Amenity flag: resource has a desktop monitor.
Sort position when listing resources. Lower values appear first.
Amenity flag: resource has a display screen.
Amenity flag: resource has a dual display screen.
Custom HTML or text included in booking confirmation emails for this resource.
Amenity flag: resource has a flip chart.
Optional grouping label used to cluster related resources together in the UI (e.g., 'Floor 2').
When true, this resource does not appear on the booking calendar view.
Amenity flag: resource has internet access.
Minimum interval (in minutes) between consecutive bookings on this resource, used as a buffer for setup or cleaning.
Amenity flag: resource has a large display.
Timestamp of the last cleaning event for this resource.
Minimum lead time (in minutes) required before a booking can start. Prevents last-minute bookings.
Cut-off in minutes before the booking start time. Cancellations after this point are considered late and may incur a fee.
GPS latitude coordinate of the resource's physical location.
When true, the total number of visitors added to a booking cannot exceed the Allocation capacity.
Comma-separated string of linked resource IDs (read-only alternative view of LinkedResources).
IDs of other resources that are automatically booked together with this one (e.g., a room and its attached breakout area).
GPS longitude coordinate of the resource's physical location.
Maximum allowed duration for a single booking on this resource, in minutes.
Minimum allowed duration for a single booking on this resource, in minutes.
Display name of the resource (e.g., 'Board Room A', 'Phone Booth 3').
Amenity flag: resource has natural light.
URL of a new image to set as the resource's picture. The image is downloaded and stored by the system.
Cooldown in minutes: prevents the same user from booking this specific resource again within this window after their last booking ends.
Cooldown in minutes: prevents the same user from booking any resource after booking this one, for the specified window.
Cooldown in minutes: prevents any user from booking this resource within the specified window after the previous booking ends.
When true, only contacts (non-member customers) can book this resource.
When true, only coworkers invoiced by this specific location can book this resource.
When true, only active members (coworkers with a plan) can book this resource.
Amenity flag: resource has a PA system.
Filename of the resource's current picture (read-only).
Amenity flag: resource has a privacy screen.
Amenity flag: resource has a projector.
Amenity flag: resource is located in a quiet zone.
Booking availability exception IDs to remove (use on update to remove without replacing).
Linked resource IDs to remove (use on update to remove without replacing).
Tariff IDs to remove from the allowed list (use on update to remove without replacing).
Team IDs to remove from the allowed list (use on update to remove without replacing).
Maximum time span (in months) over which a recurring booking series can extend.
Maximum number of occurrences allowed when creating a recurring booking for this resource.
When true, bookings for this resource are held as pending until an admin approves them.
ID of the ResourceType this resource belongs to. Determines which ExtraService pricing rules apply.
Amenity flag: resource has secure storage.
Amenity flag: resource has a security lock.
JSON-encoded shifts configuration defining the resource's availability schedule.
Amenity flag: resource has a standard phone.
Amenity flag: resource has a standing desk.
Built-in resource category used for system behaviour (e.g., MeetingRoom, HotDesk, PhoneBooth). Distinct from the custom ResourceType.
IDs of pricing plans (tariffs) whose members are allowed to book this resource. Empty means no plan restriction.
Amenity flag: tea and coffee are available.
IDs of teams whose members are allowed to book this resource. Empty means no team restriction.
When true, bookings for this resource use the location's shared Zoom account to create virtual meetings.
Amenity flag: resource has video conferencing equipment.
Whether the resource is visible and bookable by end users. Hidden resources can still be booked by admins.
Amenity flag: resource has a voice recorder.
Amenity flag: resource has a whiteboard.
Amenity flag: resource has a wireless charger.
Amenity flag: resource has wireless presentation capabilities.

Command.Name: run-command
Command.Description: Execute a command on one or more resources

Argument.Name: command-key
Argument.Description: The command key to execute

Argument.Name: ids
Argument.Description: Comma-separated entity IDs to run the command on

Option.Description: Output JSON envelope with summary for AI agents
Override the Nexudus API base URL
Command parameters in Name=Value format (can be specified multiple times)
Output JSON envelope with summary for AI agents

Override the Nexudus API base URL
Output JSON envelope with summary for AI agents
Override the Nexudus API base URL
Output JSON envelope with summary for AI agents
Override the Nexudus API base URL
Linked resource type IDs to add
Output JSON envelope with summary for AI agents
Date from which this price applies
Date until which this price applies
Override the Nexudus API base URL
Use as the default price for matched resource types
Fixed cost price applied once the threshold is reached
Only apply within the resource's available times
Price factor for average demand periods
Price factor for high demand periods
Price factor for low demand periods
Linked resource type IDs to remove
Whether the price is visible on the portal and app
Linked resource type IDs to add
Output JSON envelope with summary for AI agents
Date from which this price applies
Date until which this price applies
Override the Nexudus API base URL
Use as the default price for matched resource types
Fixed cost price applied once the threshold is reached
Only apply within the resource's available times
Price factor for average demand periods
Price factor for high demand periods

Location where this access token is valid
Free-text description of this access token

Total minutes of WiFi access included in this token

Command.Name: run-command
Command.Description: Execute a command on one or more accesstokens

Argument.Name: command-key
Argument.Description: The command key to execute

Argument.Name: ids
Argument.Description: Comma-separated entity IDs to run the command on

Option.Description: Output JSON envelope with summary for AI agents
Override the Nexudus API base URL
Command parameters in Name=Value format (can be specified multiple times)
Output JSON envelope with summary for AI agents
Override the Nexudus API base URL
Output JSON envelope with summary for AI agents
Override the Nexudus API base URL
Output JSON envelope with summary for AI agents
Override the Nexudus API base URL
Products to include with this booking (JSON array or @filepath)
Visitors to add to this booking (JSON array or @filepath)
Fixed rate (extra service) to use for pricing. If null, Nexudus calculates the rate automatically based on the resource type, booking parameters and customer
ID of a desk or unit in the floor plan to associate with this booking
Charge the booking to the customer making it rather than their paying member (if any)
Whether the booking was made in the portal or the app
Admin-set fixed price for the booking, regardless of what extra service (rate) is associated with it
Create a repeating booking series. Create-only: once created, only WhichBookingsToUpdate can be used to update the series. No new bookings can be added to the series
Tentative booking. Must be approved by an administrator before confirmed or charged. Tentative bookings still block the calendar
Action to apply when updating or deleting bookings in a repeated series. This is the only field that can modify a series after creation
Output JSON envelope with summary for AI agents
Override the Nexudus API base URL
Products to include with this booking (JSON array or @filepath)
Visitors to add to this booking (JSON array or @filepath)
Fixed rate (extra service) to use for pricing. If null, Nexudus calculates the rate automatically based on the resource type, booking parameters and customer
ID of a desk or unit in the floor plan to associate with this booking
Charge the booking to the customer making it rather than their paying member (if any)
Whether the booking was made in the portal or the app
Admin-set fixed price for the booking, regardless of what extra service (rate) is associated with it
Create a repeating booking series. Create-only: once created, only WhichBookingsToUpdate can be used to update the series. No new bookings can be added to the series
Tentative booking. Must be approved by an administrator before confirmed or charged. Tentative bookings still block the calendar
Action to apply when updating or deleting bookings in a repeated series. This is the only field that can modify a series after creation
Output JSON envelope with summary for AI agents
Override the Nexudus API base URL

Command.Name: run-command
Command.Description: Execute a command on one or more bookings

Argument.Name: command-key
Argument.Description: The command key to execute

Argument.Name: ids
Argument.Description: Comma-separated entity IDs to run the command on

Option.Description: Output JSON envelope with summary for AI agents
Override the Nexudus API base URL
Command parameters in Name=Value format (can be specified multiple times)
Output JSON envelope with summary for AI agents
Override the Nexudus API base URL
Output JSON envelope with summary for AI agents
Override the Nexudus API base URL
Whether this exception is currently enforced
Resource IDs to add to the exception
Output JSON envelope with summary for AI agents
Override the Nexudus API base URL
ID of the location this exception applies to
Whether this exception recurs annually on the same dates
Start date/time of the blocked period
Message shown to users who attempt to book a blocked resource
Resource IDs to remove from the exception
Resource IDs to block during the specified period
End date/time of the blocked period
Whether this exception is currently enforced
Resource IDs to add to the exception
Output JSON envelope with summary for AI agents
Override the Nexudus API base URL
Whether this exception recurs annually on the same dates
Start date/time of the blocked period
Message shown to users who attempt to book a blocked resource
Resource IDs to remove from the exception
Resource IDs to block during the specified period
End date/time of the blocked period
Output JSON envelope with summary for AI agents
Override the Nexudus API base URL

The ID of the booking this note belongs to
The text content of the note, visible to customers in booking confirmations

Override the Nexudus API base URL
The ID of the booking this note belongs to
The text content of the note, visible to customers in booking confirmations

Override the Nexudus API base URL

Command.Name: run-command
Command.Description: Execute a command on one or more bookingnotes

Argument.Name: command-key
Argument.Description: The command key to execute

Argument.Name: ids
Argument.Description: Comma-separated entity IDs to run the command on

Option.Description: Output JSON envelope with summary for AI agents
Override the Nexudus API base URL
Command parameters in Name=Value format (can be specified multiple times)
Output JSON envelope with summary for AI agents
Override the Nexudus API base URL
Output JSON envelope with summary for AI agents
Override the Nexudus API base URL
Output JSON envelope with summary for AI agents
Override the Nexudus API base URL
The ID of the booking to attach this product to
Whether to invoice this product based on the booking duration in minutes
The ID of the product to add. Must be linked to the booked resource via `ResourceProduct`
The number of units of this product to include in the booking
Output JSON envelope with summary for AI agents
Override the Nexudus API base URL
The ID of the booking to attach this product to
Whether to invoice this product based on the booking duration in minutes
The ID of the product to add. Must be linked to the booked resource via `ResourceProduct`
The number of units of this product to include in the booking
Output JSON envelope with summary for AI agents
Override the Nexudus API base URL

Command.Name: run-command
Command.Description: Execute a command on one or more bookingproducts

Argument.Name: command-key

Option.Description: Output JSON envelope with summary for AI agents
Override the Nexudus API base URL
Command parameters in Name=Value format (can be specified multiple times)
Output JSON envelope with summary for AI agents
Override the Nexudus API base URL
Output JSON envelope with summary for AI agents
Override the Nexudus API base URL
Output JSON envelope with summary for AI agents
Override the Nexudus API base URL
The ID of the booking this visitor is associated with. The booking must belong to a customer (CoworkerId not null).
The ID of the visitor to add as a guest to the booking.
Output JSON envelope with summary for AI agents
Override the Nexudus API base URL
The ID of the booking this visitor is associated with. The booking must belong to a customer (CoworkerId not null).
The ID of the visitor to add as a guest to the booking.
Output JSON envelope with summary for AI agents
Override the Nexudus API base URL

Command.Name: run-command
Command.Description: Execute a command on one or more bookingvisitors

Argument.Name: command-key
Argument.Description: The command key to execute

Argument.Name: ids

ID of the location where the check-in takes place
ID of the customer being checked in
Date and time the customer checked in
MAC addresses of devices detected during a network-activity check-in
How the check-in was created (e.g. Manual, DoorAccess, NetworkActivity, NexIO Tile, or Sensor)
Date and time the customer checked out. Null while the check-in is still open
Output JSON envelope with summary for AI agents
Override the Nexudus API base URL
ID of the customer being checked in
Date and time the customer checked in
MAC addresses of devices detected during a network-activity check-in
How the check-in was created (e.g. Manual, DoorAccess, NetworkActivity, NexIO Tile, or Sensor)
Date and time the customer checked out. Null while the check-in is still open
Output JSON envelope with summary for AI agents
Override the Nexudus API base URL

When true, shares the file with the customer and lists it in the portal and mobile app

When true, removes the uploaded file
When true, removes the signed copy of the document

Data URL of the file to upload
Email address to notify when the customer signs the document
When true, sends a notification to the customer asking them to electronically sign the document

Command.Name: run-command
Command.Description: Execute a command on one or more coworkers

Argument.Name: command-key
Argument.Description: The command key to execute

Argument.Name: ids
Argument.Description: Comma-separated entity IDs to run the command on

Option.Description: Output JSON envelope with summary for AI agents
Override the Nexudus API base URL
Command parameters in Name=Value format (can be specified multiple times)
Output JSON envelope with summary for AI agents
Override the Nexudus API base URL
Output JSON envelope with summary for AI agents
Override the Nexudus API base URL
Output JSON envelope with summary for AI agents
Override the Nexudus API base URL
Location where the delivery was received
Whether the enclosed check has been deposited
Date and time the check was deposited
Set to true to remove the attached file
Set to true to remove the forwarding document file
Set to true to remove the scanned file
Set to true to remove the signature file
Whether the customer has collected the delivery
Date and time the customer collected the delivery
Contract contact the delivery is addressed to (alternative to CoworkerId)
Customer the delivery is addressed to
Type of delivery item (Mail, Parcel, Check, Publicity, or Other)
URL of a file to attach to this delivery (e.g. photo or document)
Whether the delivery has been forwarded to the customer
URL of the forwarding receipt or proof-of-forwarding document
Date and time the delivery was forwarded
How the customer wants this delivery handled (e.g. StoreForCollection, Forward, Shred)
Physical location where the delivery is being stored (e.g. mailroom shelf)
Short label or description identifying the delivery item
Whether the delivery has been processed according to the handling preference
Name of the staff member who received the delivery
Whether the delivery has been recycled
Date and time the delivery was recycled
Whether the customer must sign upon collection
Whether the delivery has been returned to sender
Date and time the delivery was returned to sender
Whether the delivery contents have been scanned
URL of the scanned copy of the delivery contents
Date and time the delivery contents were scanned
Whether the delivery has been shredded
Date and time the delivery was shredded
URL of the customer's collection signature image
Whether the customer has signed for the delivery
Whether the delivery is being held for customer collection
Date and time the delivery was placed in storage for collection
Output JSON envelope with summary for AI agents
Override the Nexudus API base URL
Whether the enclosed check has been deposited
Date and time the check was deposited
Set to true to remove the attached file
Set to true to remove the forwarding document file
Set to true to remove the scanned file
Set to true to remove the signature file
Whether the customer has collected the delivery
Date and time the customer collected the delivery
Contract contact the delivery is addressed to (alternative to CoworkerId)
Customer the delivery is addressed to
Type of delivery item (Mail, Parcel, Check, Publicity, or Other)
URL of a file to attach to this delivery (e.g. photo or document)
Whether the delivery has been forwarded to the customer
URL of the forwarding receipt or proof-of-forwarding document
Date and time the delivery was forwarded
How the customer wants this delivery handled (e.g. StoreForCollection, Forward, Shred)
Physical location where the delivery is being stored (e.g. mailroom shelf)
Short label or description identifying the delivery item
Whether the delivery has been processed according to the handling preference
Name of the staff member who received the delivery
Whether the delivery has been recycled
Date and time the delivery was recycled
Whether the customer must sign upon collection
Whether the delivery has been returned to sender
Date and time the delivery was returned to sender
Whether the delivery contents have been scanned
URL of the scanned copy of the delivery contents
Date and time the delivery contents were scanned
Whether the delivery has been shredded
Date and time the delivery was shredded
URL of the customer's collection signature image
Whether the customer has signed for the delivery
Whether the delivery is being held for customer collection
Date and time the delivery was placed in storage for collection
Output JSON envelope with summary for AI agents
Override the Nexudus API base URL

ID of the location (business) this check belongs to
ID of the customer being verified
Free-text notes or details about this check
How the check is performed: Manual (operator review) or StripeIdentity (Stripe-delegated)
Expiration date of the identity document
Authority or organisation that issued the identity document
Number or identifier on the identity document
Type of identity document used when VerificationType is IdDocument
Display name for this verification check
Current status of the verification (Pending, Submitted, Successful, Failed, or Cancelled)
Whether this check verifies the customer's identity (IdDocument) or address (Address)
Type of address document used when VerificationType is Address
Output JSON envelope with summary for AI agents
Override the Nexudus API base URL
ID of the customer being verified
Free-text notes or details about this check
How the check is performed: Manual (operator review) or StripeIdentity (Stripe-delegated)
Expiration date of the identity document
Authority or organisation that issued the identity document
Number or identifier on the identity document
Type of identity document used when VerificationType is IdDocument
Display name for this verification check
Current status of the verification (Pending, Submitted, Successful, Failed, or Cancelled)
Whether this check verifies the customer's identity (IdDocument) or address (Address)
Output JSON envelope with summary for AI agents
Override the Nexudus API base URL

ID of the location this asset belongs to
Set to true to remove the current asset image

Display name of the asset (e.g. 'Standing Desk #3', 'HP LaserJet')
Public URL of an image to upload as the asset photo
ID of the bookable resource this asset is linked to (used when AssignToType = Resource)
Stock-keeping unit code for the asset

Command.Name: run-command
Command.Description: Execute a command on one or more msofficeadmincalendars

Argument.Name: command-key
Argument.Description: The command key to execute

Argument.Name: ids
Argument.Description: Comma-separated entity IDs to run the command on

Option.Description: Output JSON envelope with summary for AI agents
Override the Nexudus API base URL
Command parameters in Name=Value format (can be specified multiple times)
Output JSON envelope with summary for AI agents
Override the Nexudus API base URL
Output JSON envelope with summary for AI agents
Override the Nexudus API base URL
Whether this rule is currently active and evaluated during booking.
Allowed tariff IDs to add (use on update to append without replacing).
Allowed team IDs to add (use on update to append without replacing).
Course IDs to add to the scoped list (use on update to append without replacing).
Event category IDs to add to the scoped list (use on update to append without replacing).
Member IDs to add to the scoped list (use on update to append without replacing).
Tariff IDs to add to the scoped list (use on update to append without replacing).
Team IDs to add to the scoped list (use on update to append without replacing).
Output JSON envelope with summary for AI agents
IDs of pricing plans whose customers are allowed to book when this rule applies to them. Only customers on one of these plans can proceed.
IDs of teams whose members are allowed to book when this rule applies to them. Only members of one of these teams can proceed.
Date from which this rule starts being evaluated. Null means no start-date restriction.
Date after which this rule stops being evaluated. Null means no end-date restriction.
Override the Nexudus API base URL
Maximum number of days in advance a booking can be made. Null means no limit.
Fixed cancellation fee amount. Used when CancellationFeeType is Absolute.
Cancellation fee as a percentage of the booking cost. Used when CancellationFeeType is Percentage.
ID of the product used to invoice cancellation fees when this rule applies.
How the cancellation fee is calculated: Absolute (fixed amount) or Percentage (of booking cost).
When true, a fee is charged for late cancellations (past the LateCancellationLimit).
IDs of courses this rule applies to. The rule only fires for customers who have completed one of these courses.
Time slots defining when this rule applies (eligibility windows). The year, month and day component of FromTime/ToTime is always 1976-01-01. (JSON array or @filepath)
Order in which this rule is evaluated relative to other rules on the same resource. Lower values are evaluated first.
IDs of event categories this rule applies to. The rule only fires for customers who have attended (checked in to) an event in one of these categories.
Minimum interval (in minutes) between consecutive bookings on this resource, used as a buffer for setup or cleaning.
Minimum lead time (in minutes) required before a booking can start. Prevents last-minute bookings.
Cut-off in minutes before the booking start time. Cancellations after this point are considered late and may incur a fee.
Maximum allowed duration for a single booking, in minutes.
IDs of specific members this rule applies to. When set, the rule only fires for these individual members.
Minimum allowed duration for a single booking, in minutes.
Cooldown in minutes: prevents the same user from booking this specific resource again within this window after their last booking ends.
Cooldown in minutes: prevents the same user from booking any resource after booking this one, for the specified window.
Cooldown in minutes: prevents any user from booking this resource within the specified window after the previous booking ends.
When true, this rule applies only to contacts (non-member customers).
When true, this rule applies only to active members (coworkers with a plan).
Message shown to the user when their booking is rejected by this rule.
Allowed tariff IDs to remove (use on update to remove without replacing).
Allowed team IDs to remove (use on update to remove without replacing).
Course IDs to remove from the scoped list (use on update to remove without replacing).
Event category IDs to remove from the scoped list (use on update to remove without replacing).
Member IDs to remove from the scoped list (use on update to remove without replacing).
Tariff IDs to remove from the scoped list (use on update to remove without replacing).
Team IDs to remove from the scoped list (use on update to remove without replacing).
Maximum time span (in months) over which a recurring booking series can extend under this rule.
Maximum number of occurrences allowed when creating a recurring booking under this rule.
When true, no further rules are evaluated after this one matches.
IDs of pricing plans this rule applies to. When set, the rule only fires for customers on one of these plans.
IDs of teams this rule applies to. When set, the rule only fires for members of one of these teams.
The days and times the resources can be booked when this rule applies. The year, month and day component of FromTime/ToTime is always 1976-01-01. (JSON array or @filepath)
Whether this rule is currently active and evaluated during booking.
Allowed tariff IDs to add (use on update to append without replacing).
Allowed team IDs to add (use on update to append without replacing).
Course IDs to add to the scoped list (use on update to append without replacing).
Event category IDs to add to the scoped list (use on update to append without replacing).
Member IDs to add to the scoped list (use on update to append without replacing).
Tariff IDs to add to the scoped list (use on update to append without replacing).
Team IDs to add to the scoped list (use on update to append without replacing).
Output JSON envelope with summary for AI agents
IDs of pricing plans whose customers are allowed to book when this rule applies to them. Only customers on one of these plans can proceed.
IDs of teams whose members are allowed to book when this rule applies to them. Only members of one of these teams can proceed.
Date from which this rule starts being evaluated. Null means no start-date restriction.
Date after which this rule stops being evaluated. Null means no end-date restriction.
Override the Nexudus API base URL
Maximum number of days in advance a booking can be made. Null means no limit.
Fixed cancellation fee amount. Used when CancellationFeeType is Absolute.
Cancellation fee as a percentage of the booking cost. Used when CancellationFeeType is Percentage.
ID of the product used to invoice cancellation fees when this rule applies.
How the cancellation fee is calculated: Absolute (fixed amount) or Percentage (of booking cost).
When true, a fee is charged for late cancellations (past the LateCancellationLimit).
IDs of courses this rule applies to. The rule only fires for customers who have completed one of these courses.
Time slots defining when this rule applies (eligibility windows). The year, month and day component of FromTime/ToTime is always 1976-01-01. (JSON array or @filepath)
Order in which this rule is evaluated relative to other rules on the same resource. Lower values are evaluated first.
IDs of event categories this rule applies to. The rule only fires for customers who have attended (checked in to) an event in one of these categories.
Minimum interval (in minutes) between consecutive bookings on this resource, used as a buffer for setup or cleaning.
Minimum lead time (in minutes) required before a booking can start. Prevents last-minute bookings.
Cut-off in minutes before the booking start time. Cancellations after this point are considered late and may incur a fee.
Maximum allowed duration for a single booking, in minutes.
IDs of specific members this rule applies to. When set, the rule only fires for these individual members.
Minimum allowed duration for a single booking, in minutes.
Cooldown in minutes: prevents the same user from booking this specific resource again within this window after their last booking ends.
Cooldown in minutes: prevents the same user from booking any resource after booking this one, for the specified window.
Cooldown in minutes: prevents any user from booking this resource within the specified window after the previous booking ends.
When true, this rule applies only to contacts (non-member customers).
When true, this rule applies only to active members (coworkers with a plan).
Message shown to the user when their booking is rejected by this rule.
Allowed tariff IDs to remove (use on update to remove without replacing).
Allowed team IDs to remove (use on update to remove without replacing).
Course IDs to remove from the scoped list (use on update to remove without replacing).
Event category IDs to remove from the scoped list (use on update to remove without replacing).
Member IDs to remove from the scoped list (use on update to remove without replacing).
Tariff IDs to remove from the scoped list (use on update to remove without replacing).
Team IDs to remove from the scoped list (use on update to remove without replacing).
Maximum time span (in months) over which a recurring booking series can extend under this rule.
Maximum number of occurrences allowed when creating a recurring booking under this rule.
When true, no further rules are evaluated after this one matches.
IDs of pricing plans this rule applies to. When set, the rule only fires for customers on one of these plans.
IDs of teams this rule applies to. When set, the rule only fires for members of one of these teams.
The days and times the resources can be booked when this rule applies. The year, month and day component of FromTime/ToTime is always 1976-01-01. (JSON array or @filepath)
Output JSON envelope with summary for AI agents
Override the Nexudus API base URL

Command.Name: run-command
Command.Description: Execute a command on one or more teams

Argument.Name: command-key
Argument.Description: The command key to execute

Argument.Name: ids
Argument.Description: Comma-separated entity IDs to run the command on

Option.Description: Output JSON envelope with summary for AI agents
Override the Nexudus API base URL
Command parameters in Name=Value format (can be specified multiple times)
